title_en: Transactions and Marriage in Bidayat al-Mubtadi
title_id: Muamalah dan Pernikahan dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi
content_id:
Bagian-bagian muamalah (transaksi) dan nikah (pernikahan) dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi menyajikan hukum niaga dan keluarga mazhab Hanafi dalam format ringkas yang sama yang mencirikan seluruh karya. Bagian-bagian ini menjadi dasar bagi pembahasan luas dalam Al-Hidayah yang tetap menjadi referensi Hanafi utama untuk hukum niaga dan status pribadi.

Dalam hukum jual beli (buyu'), mazhab Hanafi mensyaratkan beberapa kondisi untuk akad yang sah: kecakapan hukum kedua belah pihak, keberadaan barang yang dijual pada saat akad, kemampuan penyerahannya, penentuan harga, dan ketiadaan ketidakpastian berlebihan (gharar). Bidayat al-Mubtadi menyajikan syarat-syarat ini secara ringkas. Posisi mazhab Hanafi tentang keabsahan akad opsi (khiyar al-syart — periode opsi yang disepakati kedua belah pihak, di mana salah satunya dapat membatalkan) lebih permisif dari beberapa mazhab lain, membolehkan periode opsi hingga tiga hari berdasarkan preseden Nabi.

Perlakuan mazhab Hanafi terhadap riba dirinci dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi mengikuti kerangka berbasis hadis yang sama tentang enam komoditas ribawi (emas, perak, gandum, jelai, kurma, garam). Mazhab Hanafi berpendapat bahwa 'illah (penyebab hukum yang efektif) untuk riba dalam komoditas-komoditas ini adalah bahwa komoditas-komoditas tersebut dijual berdasarkan takaran (kayl) atau timbangan (wazn) dan sejenis. 'Illah ini — takaran/timbangan dan sejenis — menentukan komoditas-komoditas lain mana yang secara analogis tunduk pada pembatasan riba. Mazhab Hanafi memperluas pembatasan riba lebih luas dari beberapa mazhab lain berdasarkan 'illah yang berlaku luas ini.

Akad nikah dibahas dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi dengan posisi-posisi khas mazhab Hanafi. Yang paling patut dicatat, mazhab Hanafi tidak mensyaratkan kehadiran wali sebagai syarat keabsahan pernikahan seorang perempuan dewasa yang berakal sehat. Seorang perempuan dewasa yang berakal sehat dapat mengakadkan pernikahannya sendiri tanpa keterlibatan wali — sebuah posisi yang secara signifikan lebih permisif dari persyaratan mazhab Syafi'i yang mensyaratkan wali bagi semua perempuan dan persyaratan mazhab Maliki untuk perempuan perawan. Dasar evidensial posisi Hanafi ini — ayat-ayat Al-Qur'an yang membahas perempuan yang 'mencegah diri mereka dari pernikahan' dan prinsip umum kecakapan hukum orang dewasa — dibiarkan untuk Al-Hidayah yang mengembangkannya.

Mahar disajikan dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi sebagai hak wajib istri. Mazhab Hanafi menetapkan mahar minimum sepuluh dirham — di bawah jumlah ini, akad nikah tidak batal tetapi istri berhak atas jumlah minimum terlepas dari apa yang disyaratkan. Mahar menjadi milik eksklusif istri saat konsumasi (atau kematian suami), dan suami tidak berhak atasnya dalam keadaan apapun.

Hukum perceraian dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi mencerminkan posisi-posisi mazhab Hanafi tentang talak: talak tiga kali oleh suami berlaku sebagai perceraian tiga kali meskipun diucapkan dalam satu sesi — sebuah posisi yang dipegang oleh Hanafi dan beberapa mazhab lain tetapi diperdebatkan oleh yang lain yang berpendapat bahwa tiga ucapan dalam satu sesi dihitung sebagai hanya satu talak raj'i. Ini telah menjadi salah satu isu yang paling diperdebatkan dalam hukum keluarga Muslim kontemporer.

Peran Bidayat al-Mubtadi dalam pendidikan hukum Hanafi tidak terpisahkan dari Al-Hidayah — kedua teks tersebut dipelajari bersama-sama, dan penguasaan pelajar atas primer memungkinkan syarh berfungsi sebagaimana yang dirancang. Pencapaian al-Marghinani dalam Bidayat al-Mubtadi adalah menciptakan kerangka yang proporsional sempurna: cukup komprehensif untuk mencakup semua bidang utama hukum Hanafi, cukup ringkas untuk dikuasai sebelum melanjutkan ke syarh.
content_en:
The mu'amalat (transactions) and nikah (marriage) sections of Bidayat al-Mubtadi present the Hanafi school's commercial and family law in the same concise format that characterizes the entire work. These sections formed the basis for the expansive treatment in Al-Hidayah that remains the primary Hanafi reference for commercial and personal status law.

In the law of sales (buyU'), the Hanafi school requires several conditions for a valid contract: legal capacity of both parties, the existence of the subject matter at the time of contract, its deliverability, the specification of price, and the absence of excessive uncertainty (gharar). Bidayat al-Mubtadi presents these conditions concisely. The Hanafi school's position on the validity of the contract of options (khiyar al-shart — the option period agreed by both parties, during which either may cancel) is more permissive than some other schools, allowing option periods of up to three days by prophetic precedent.

The Hanafi school's treatment of riba is detailed in Bidayat al-Mubtadi following the same hadith-based framework of the six ribawi goods (gold, silver, wheat, barley, dates, salt). The Hanafi school holds that the 'illah (effective cause) for riba in these goods is that they are sold by measure (kayl) or weight (wazn) and are of the same genus. This 'illah — measure/weight and same-genus — determines which other goods are analogically subject to riba restrictions. The Hanafi school extends riba restrictions more broadly than some other schools on the basis of this widely-applicable 'illah.

The contract of marriage (nikah) is treated in Bidayat al-Mubtadi with the Hanafi school's distinctive positions. Most notably, the Hanafi school does not require the presence of a wali (guardian) as a condition for the validity of the marriage of a sane, adult woman. An adult woman of sound mind may contract her own marriage without a guardian's involvement — a significantly more permissive position than the Shafi'i school's requirement of a wali for all women and the Maliki school's requirement for virgins. The evidential basis for this Hanafi position — the Quranic verses addressing women who 'prevent themselves from marriage' and the general principle of adult legal capacity — is left for Al-Hidayah to develop.

The mahr (dower) is presented in Bidayat al-Mubtadi as an obligatory right of the wife. The Hanafi school specifies a minimum mahr of ten dirhams — below this amount, the marriage contract is not void but the wife is entitled to the minimum regardless of what was stipulated. The mahr becomes the wife's exclusive property upon consummation (or death of the husband), and the husband has no claim to it under any circumstances.

Divorce law in Bidayat al-Mubtadi reflects the Hanafi school's positions on talaq: the husband's three-fold divorce is effective as a triple divorce even if pronounced in a single session — a position held by the Hanafi and some other schools but contested by others who hold that three pronouncements in one session count as only one revocable divorce. This has been one of the most debated issues in contemporary Muslim family law.

Bidayat al-Mubtadi's role in Hanafi legal education is inseparable from Al-Hidayah — the two texts are studied together, and the student's mastery of the primer enables the commentary to function as it was designed. Al-Marghinani's achievement in Bidayat al-Mubtadi was to create a perfectly proportioned framework: comprehensive enough to cover all major areas of Hanafi law, compressed enough to be mastered before proceeding to the commentary.
content_ar: The mu'amalat (transactions) and nikah (marriage) sections of Bidayat al-Mubtadi present the الحنفي school's commercial and الأسرة law in the same concise format that characterizes the entire work. These sections formed the basis for the expansive treatment in Al-Hidayah that remains the الأساسي الحنفي reference for commercial and personal status law. In the law of sales (buyU'), the الحنفي school requires several conditions for a valid contract: legal capacity of both parties, the existence of the subject matter at the time of contract, its deliverability, the specification of price, and the absence of excessive uncertainty (gharar). Bidayat al-Mubtadi presents these conditions concisely. The الحنفي school's position on the validity of the contract of options (khiyar al-shart — the option period agreed by both parties, خلال which either may cancel) is more permissive than some other schools, allowing option periods of up to three days by prophetic precedent. The الحنفي school's treatment of riba is detailed in Bidayat al-Mubtadi following the same الحديث-based framework of the six ribawi goods (gold, silver, wheat, barley, dates, salt). The الحنفي school holds that the 'illah (effective cause) for riba in these goods is that they are sold by measure (kayl) or weight (wazn) and are of the same genus. This 'illah — measure/weight and same-genus — determines which other goods are analogically subject to riba restrictions. The الحنفي school extends riba restrictions more broadly than some other schools on the basis of this widely-applicable 'illah. The contract of marriage (nikah) is treated in Bidayat al-Mubtadi with the الحنفي school's distinctive positions. Most notably, the الحنفي school does not require the presence of a wali (guardian) as a condition for the validity of the marriage of a sane, adult woman. An adult woman of sound العقل may contract her own marriage without a guardian's involvement — a significantly more permissive position than the الشافعي school's requirement of a wali for all women and the المالكي school's requirement for virgins. The evidential basis for this الحنفي position — the القرآني الآيات addressing women who 'prevent themselves from marriage' and the general principle of adult legal capacity — is left for Al-Hidayah to develop. The mahr (dower) is presented in Bidayat al-Mubtadi as an obligatory الحق of the الزوجة. The الحنفي school specifies a minimum mahr of ten dirhams — below this amount, the marriage contract is not void but the الزوجة is entitled to the minimum regardless of what was stipulated. The mahr becomes the الزوجة's exclusive property upon consummation (or الموت of the الزوج), and the الزوج has no claim to it under any circumstances. Divorce law in Bidayat al-Mubtadi reflects the الحنفي school's positions on talaq: the الزوج's three-fold divorce is effective as a triple divorce even إذا pronounced in a single session — a position held by the الحنفي and some other
